creator: steinhelten@gmail.com
text:
Stranger and stranger.  I tried to do a minimal case to recreate the problem, which I did, but then I ran into a problem with pkg.

I can reproduce the original problem easily.  As you can see, sendmail identifies itself as Version 8.18.1 immediately after the first boot after creating the image, yet it exhibits this behavior.  What I didn't know before is that while "newaliases" fails, "sendmail -bi" fails.  I still consider it a bug, of course, that "newaliases" fails.

Also, note that the "newaliases" syntax error message is off by one line - it's actually line 79 that causes it to get upset though it says 78.  It also says "no such file or directory" which is misleading.

To create the SD image
======================
# xzcat FreeBSD-14.1-RELEASE-arm64-aarch64-RPI.img.xz | dd of=/dev/da0 bs=1m

After boot
==========
# uname -a
FreeBSD generic 14.1-RELEASE FreeBSD 14.1-RELEASE releng/14.1-n267679-10e31f0946d8 GENERIC arm64
freebsd@generic:~ % /usr/libexec/sendmail/sendmail -d0.101
Version 8.18.1
# newaliases
[ Succeeds ]
# cat <<EOF >>/etc/aliases
> msgs:  "| /usr/bin/msgs -s"
> csadmin:        "John Radical" <jrad@gmail.com>,
>         "John Lind" <john@starfire.mn.org>
> EOF
# newaliases
aliases line 78: syntax error
newaliases: could not parse aliases file `/etc/aliases': No such file or directory
# /usr/libexec/sendmail/sendmail -bi
WARNING: local host name (generic) is not qualified; see cf/README: WHO AM I?
/etc/mail/aliases: 31 aliases, longest 74 bytes, 402 bytes total


Then I brought the system up to current
================
# freebsd-update fetch
... (yadda yadda)
# freebsd-update install
...
# shutdown -r


After the reboot, I got the same behavior from newaliases, but here's where the story takes a bizarre turn.  I tried to install pkg to see if I could update sendmail, and I got a whole new error.
# newaliases
aliases line 78: syntax error
newaliases: could not parse aliases file `/etc/aliases': No such file or directory
# pkg
The package management tool is not yet installed on your system.
Do you want to fetch and install it now? [y/N]: yes
Bootstrapping pkg from pkg+https://pkg.FreeBSD.org/FreeBSD:14:aarch64/quarterly, please wait...
Certificate verification failed for /CN=pkg.freebsd.org
0020E18A572F0000:error:0A000086:SSL routines:tls_post_process_server_certificate:certificate verify failed:/usr/src/crypto/openssl/ssl/statem/statem_clnt.c:1890:
Certificate verification failed for /CN=pkg.freebsd.org
0020E18A572F0000:error:0A000086:SSL routines:tls_post_process_server_certificate:certificate verify failed:/usr/src/crypto/openssl/ssl/statem/statem_clnt.c:1890:
Certificate verification failed for /CN=pkg.freebsd.org
0020E18A572F0000:error:0A000086:SSL routines:tls_post_process_server_certificate:certificate verify failed:/usr/src/crypto/openssl/ssl/statem/statem_clnt.c:1890:
Certificate verification failed for /CN=pkg.freebsd.org
0020E18A572F0000:error:0A000086:SSL routines:tls_post_process_server_certificate:certificate verify failed:/usr/src/crypto/openssl/ssl/statem/statem_clnt.c:1890:
Certificate verification failed for /CN=pkg.freebsd.org
0020E18A572F0000:error:0A000086:SSL routines:tls_post_process_server_certificate:certificate verify failed:/usr/src/crypto/openssl/ssl/statem/statem_clnt.c:1890:
Certificate verification failed for /CN=pkg.freebsd.org
0020E18A572F0000:error:0A000086:SSL routines:tls_post_process_server_certificate:certificate verify failed:/usr/src/crypto/openssl/ssl/statem/statem_clnt.c:1890:
pkg: Error fetching https://pkg.FreeBSD.org/FreeBSD:14:aarch64/quarterly/Latest/pkg.txz: Authentication error
A pre-built version of pkg could not be found for your system.
Consider changing PACKAGESITE or installing it from ports: 'ports-mgmt/pkg'.
# 

So - now what?  Assume it was some sort of fluke and start over?  Or is this a whole new bug?  This did not happen to me with the first system I built, the one on which I first saw this error.
